from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

import httpx


class APIError(Exception):
    """Raised when the remote API returns a 4xx or 5xx status."""
    def __init__(self, status_code: int, body: Optional[str] = None):
        super().__init__(f'API error {status_code}: {body}')
        self.status_code = status_code
        self.body = body


class APIClient:
    """Async API client. Wraps httpx.AsyncClient."""

    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: float = 5.0):
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
        self.timeout = timeout

    async def fetch_user(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """GET /users/{user_id} → JSON dict. Raises APIError on 4xx/5xx,
        re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/users/{user_id}')
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def create_post(self, author_id: int, title: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """POST /posts. Body must be {author_id, title}. Returns the created post."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.post(
                f'{self.base_url}/posts',
                json={'author_id': author_id, 'title': title},
            )
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def ping(self) -> bool:
        """GET /healthz → True iff response is 200."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            try:
                r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/healthz')
                return r.status_code == 200
            except httpx.TransportError:
                return False
post_state_files.test_client.py:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch, MagicMock
from client import APIClient, APIError


class TestFetchUser:
    """Tests for APIClient.fetch_user method."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_fetch_user_success(self):
        """Test successful user fetch returns JSON dict."""
        client = APIClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
        
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 200
        mock_response.json.return_value = {"id": 1, "name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com"}
        
        with patch("httpx.AsyncClient") as mock_async_client:
            mock_instance = AsyncMock()
            mock_instance.get.return_value = mock_response
            mock_async_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_instance
            
            result = await client.fetch_user(user_id=1)
            
            assert result == {"id": 1, "name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com"}
            mock_instance.get.assert_called_once_with("https://api.example.com/users/1")

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_fetch_user_4xx_error(self):
        """Test 4xx response raises APIError."""
        client = APIClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
        
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 404
        mock_response.text = "User not found"
        
        with patch("httpx.AsyncClient") as mock_async_client:
            mock_instance = AsyncMock()
            mock_instance.get.return_value = mock_response
            mock_async_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_instance
            
            with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
                await client.fetch_user(user_id=999)
            
            assert exc_info.value.status_code == 404
            assert exc_info.value.body == "User not found"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_fetch_user_network_error(self):
        """Test network failure re-raises TransportError."""
        import httpx
        
        client = APIClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
        
        with patch("httpx.AsyncClient") as mock_async_client:
            mock_instance = AsyncMock()
            mock_instance.get.side_effect = httpx.TransportError("Connection refused")
            mock_async_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_instance
            
            with pytest.raises(httpx.TransportError):
                await client.fetch_user(user_id=1)


class TestCreatePost:
    """Tests for APIClient.create_post method."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_create_post_success(self):
        """Test successful post creation returns JSON dict."""
        client = APIClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
        
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 201
        mock_response.json.return_value = {"id": 42, "author_id": 1, "title": "Hello World"}
        
        with patch("httpx.AsyncClient") as mock_async_client:
            mock_instance = AsyncMock()
            mock_instance.post.return_value = mock_response
            mock_async_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_instance
            
            result = await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="Hello World")
            
            assert result == {"id": 42, "author_id": 1, "title": "Hello World"}
            mock_instance.post.assert_called_once_with(
                "https://api.example.com/posts",
                json={"author_id": 1, "title": "Hello World"}
            )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_create_post_4xx_error(self):
        """Test 4xx response raises APIError."""
        client = APIClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
        
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 400
        mock_response.text = "Title is required"
        
        with patch("httpx.AsyncClient") as mock_async_client:
            mock_instance = AsyncMock()
            mock_instance.post.return_value = mock_response
            mock_async_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_instance
            
            with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
                await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="")
            
            assert exc_info.value.status_code == 400
            assert exc_info.value.body == "Title is required"


class TestPing:
    """Tests for APIClient.ping method."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_ping_success(self):
        """Test health check returns True on 200 response."""
        client = APIClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
        
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 200
        
        with patch("httpx.AsyncClient") as mock_async_client:
            mock_instance = AsyncMock()
            mock_instance.get.return_value = mock_response
            mock_async_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_instance
            
            result = await client.ping()
            
            assert result is True

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_ping_non_200(self):
        """Test health check returns False on non-200 response."""
        client = APIClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
        
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 503
        
        with patch("httpx.AsyncClient") as mock_async_client:
            mock_instance = AsyncMock()
            mock_instance.get.return_value = mock_response
            mock_async_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_instance
            
            result = await client.ping()
            
            assert result is False

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_ping_network_error(self):
        """Test health check returns False on network error."""
        import httpx
        
        client = APIClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
        
        with patch("httpx.AsyncClient") as mock_async_client:
            mock_instance = AsyncMock()
            mock_instance.get.side_effect = httpx.TransportError("Connection timeout")
            mock_async_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_instance
            
            result = await client.ping()
            
            assert result is False


class TestAPIError:
    """Tests for APIError exception class."""

    def test_api_error_attributes(self):
        """Test APIError stores status_code and body."""
        error = APIError(404, "Not found")
        
        assert error.status_code == 404
        assert error.body == "Not found"
        assert "404" in str(error)
        assert "Not found" in str(error)

    def test_api_error_without_body(self):
        """Test APIError works without body."""
        error = APIError(500)
        
        assert error.status_code == 500
        assert error.body is None
